context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Emily Rodriguez  
- **Age:** 42  
- **Gender:** Female  
- **Experience level:** **Advanced** — 5 years serious/structured marathon training; 8 marathons completed. ~2 years consistent strength training with solid mechanics; strength work is performance/injury-prevention focused rather than PR-driven.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Marathon performance:** run **sub-3:15** (Boston Qualifier target) at the **June 14** marathon.
  - Maintain durability while increasing run volume from **~55 mpw → 65–70 mpw** (build phase).
- **Secondary goals:**
  - **Injury prevention** for running (reduce recurrence risk: IT band, plantar fascia).
  - Improve running economy/robustness via targeted strength (hips/glutes/calves/core), without compromising key run sessions.
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **June 14 — Marathon** (target **<3:15**).  
- **Context note:** Strength training is **supplementary to running**, not the primary performance metric focus.

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days (strength):** **3x/week** — **Tue / Thu / Sat**
- **Timing windows:**
  - **Tue & Thu:** **6:30 PM** (after easy run)
  - **Sat:** **8:00 AM** (after long run)
- **Session duration:** **45–60 minutes max**
- **Key constraint (recovery):** Must **not be sore** for **Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun** key run workouts (priority days for running quality/longer sessions).
- **Current run volume:** **~55 miles/week**, building toward **65–70 mpw**.

### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Home gym (converted garage)
- **Equipment available (verified list):**
  - Rogue squat rack + **pull-up bar**
  - **Olympic barbell**
  - **Plates up to 225 lb total**
  - **Dumbbells:** 10–40 lb pairs
  - **Kettlebells:** 16 / 24 / 32 kg
  - **Resistance bands**
  - **Adjustable bench**

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Prone to **right IT band syndrome flare-ups** if leg volume/load is too high — history 2022–2023, improved with PT + strength (**since 2022-01-01; monitor ongoing**).  
- **[ACTIVE]** **Tight hip flexors** (date not specified; treat as ongoing **since 2026-02-27** based on intake).  
- **[RESOLVED]** **Plantar fasciitis (2021)** — fully healed (**resolved 2021-12-31**).

### Preferences
- **Training priority:** Running performance first; strength supports durability and economy.
- **Programming preference implied by schedule:** Low-to-moderate soreness approach; avoid heavy eccentric loading that interferes with Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun runs.
- **Lift focus areas:** Hips/glutes (abduction/extension), single-leg stability, calf/foot strength, trunk control; maintain basic total-body strength.

---

## METRICS
*(Ordered by primary modality: endurance → strength.)*

### Endurance
- **Marathon PR:** **3:25:17** *(as of 2026-02-27; date of PR not provided)*  
- **Marathons completed:** **8** *(as of 2026-02-27)*  
- **Current weekly mileage:** **~55 mpw** *(as of 2026-02-27)*  
- **Planned build:** **65–70 mpw** peak *(target during current cycle; as of 2026-02-27)*  
- **Target race:** **June 14 marathon — goal sub-3:15** *(as of 2026-02-27)*

### Strength
- **Training frequency:** 3 sessions/week (Tue/Thu/Sat) *(as of 2026-02-27)*  
- **Current lift numbers:** Not provided *(needed for baseline: e.g., squat/hinge/push/pull or key accessories; track working sets rather than 1RMs if preferred).*

### Movement Quality
- **Key considerations:** History of ITB symptoms (right), tight hip flexors *(as of 2026-02-27)*  
- **Objective screens/ROM:** Not provided (e.g., single-leg squat quality, hip extension, ankle dorsiflexion).

### Body Composition
- **Weight / BF%:** Not provided.

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Intake / Profile build (Marathon + supplementary strength)
- Added identity and experience context (advanced marathon runner; strength supplementary).
- Set primary goal: **sub-3:15** at **June 14 marathon**; noted PR **3:25:17**.
- Recorded run volume **~55 mpw** building to **65–70 mpw**.
- Captured strength schedule constraints: **Tue/Thu 6:30 PM**, **Sat 8 AM**, **45–60 min**, must avoid soreness impacting **Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun** key runs.
- Documented constraints: **right IT band syndrome history (flare-prone)**, **tight hip flexors (active)**, **plantar fasciitis resolved (2021)**.
- Logged home gym equipment inventory (rack, barbell/plates, DBs, KBs, bands, bench).

--- 

**Missing data to fully validate/complete metrics (optional to provide):** date of marathon PR, recent tune-up race times (10K/half), typical easy/threshold pace or HR zones, and a few current “working set” strength numbers (e.g., split squat, RDL/hinge, calf raise, pull-up/row, press).</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Marathon-Support Strength Plan — Sub-3:15 Build (Durability + Economy)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Emily Rodriguez
- **Goal:** Improve marathon performance (sub-3:15 on June 14) while building durability and supporting mileage increase (55 → 65–70 mpw) without compromising key run sessions
- **Duration:** Fixed — **Feb 27 → Jun 14** (Race on Jun 14)

---

## Program Philosophy
Emily is an advanced marathoner whose strength work must **support** (not compete with) key running sessions and rising weekly mileage. This program uses **3 short, low-soreness sessions (Tue/Thu/Sat)** built around running-specific priorities: **hip/glute control (ITB risk reduction), calf/foot capacity (plantar fascia insurance), trunk stiffness, and single-leg stability**—with just enough heavy work to maintain strength and economy. Lower-body loading is biased toward **moderate RPE, controlled eccentrics, isometrics, and unilateral patterns** to reduce DOMS and protect Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun run quality. Progression is **autoregulated (RPE-based) with small weekly advances**, plus planned deloads every ~4th week and a taper into race day.

---

# Phase 1: Tissue Tolerance + Mechanics (Weeks 1–4)
**Objective:** Establish consistent strength stimulus with minimal soreness; reinforce hip control (esp. right side), calf/foot capacity, and trunk strength while mileage begins climbing.

### Weekly Pattern

**Tue — “Hip Dominant + Trunk (Moderate)”**
- **Focus:** Posterior chain strength + frontal-plane hip stability (ITB prevention)
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1. **Romanian Deadlift (barbell)**: 3 × 6 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Posterior-chain strength with low knee stress; good transfer to running economy.*
2. **Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (DBs)**: 2–3 × 6/side @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Single-leg strength + pelvis control without high spinal loading.*
3. **Banded Lateral Walks**: 2 × 12–15 steps/side  
   *Glute med endurance for femur control (ITB risk reducer).*
4. **Side Plank + Top-Leg Abduction**: 2 × 20–30s/side  
   *Trunk + glute med integration under fatigue.*
5. **Single-Leg Calf Raise (DB held contralateral)**: 3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 7**  
   *Calf capacity + Achilles/foot resilience.*
6. **Short-Foot Drill / Toe Yoga**: 2 × 30–45s/side  
   *Foot intrinsic strength for long-run durability.*

**Thu — “Upper + Running-Posture Core (Light Legs)”**
- **Focus:** Upper strength maintenance + anti-rotation/extension core; minimal leg fatigue
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1. **Pull-ups (or band-assisted)**: 3 × 4–8 @ **RPE 7**  
   *Lats/scap strength to maintain posture late in the marathon.*
2. **DB Bench Press**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7**
3. **1-Arm DB Row (bench-supported)**: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7**
4. **Half-Kneeling Overhead Press (DB)**: 2 × 8/side @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Half-kneeling adds pelvis control and reduces lumbar compensation.*
5. **Pallof Press (band)**: 3 × 10–12/side (1–2s hold each rep)  
6. **Hip Flexor Mobility “Finisher”** (2 rounds, easy breathing):
   - **Couch stretch**: 45–60s/side  
   - **Glute bridge iso hold**: 30–40s  
   *Addresses tight hip flexors without adding soreness.*

**Sat — “Post-Long Run Reset (Very Low Soreness)”**
- **Focus:** Restore hips/feet/core after long run; no heavy eccentrics
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets (short session)

1. **Hip Thrust (barbell or DB)**: 2–3 × 8 @ **RPE 6**  
   *Glute drive with limited DOMS compared to deep squatting patterns.*
2. **Step-Down (low box) OR Supported Single-Leg Squat to box**: 2 × 6–8/side @ **RPE 6**  
   *Eccentric control for knee/hip tracking (ITB-friendly if kept controlled).*
3. **Tibialis Raises (against wall or band)**: 2 × 15–20  
   *Lower-leg balance for foot/ankle resilience.*
4. **Farmer Carry (DB/KB)**: 4 × 30–45s @ steady effort  
   *Trunk stiffness + gait/posture under load.*
5. **Optional if time:** **Adductor side plank (Copenhagen, short lever)** 1–2 × 15–25s/side  
   *Adductor strength can support pelvic control and knee tracking.*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main lifts (RDL, RFESS, pull-ups/presses):** Progress via **double progression**.
  - Keep load the same until you hit the **top of the rep range** at the target RPE, then add **2.5–10 lb** next week.
- **Accessory hip/calf/core:** Add **1–2 reps per set** weekly or a small load bump while keeping form crisp.
- **Deload (Week 4):** Reduce total sets by **~40–50%**; keep intensity ~same or slightly lighter (**RPE 6**).  
- **Advance to Phase 2** if: minimal lingering soreness, run quality is unaffected, and you’re completing sets at prescribed RPE.

---

# Phase 2: Strength Maintenance + Power Endurance (Weeks 5–10)
**Objective:** Maintain/very slightly build strength while mileage peaks; shift lower body toward **higher specificity** (stiffness, single-leg robustness, calf endurance) and keep soreness tightly managed.

### Weekly Pattern

**Tue — “Hinge + Split Squat (Moderate-Heavy, Low Volume)”**
- **Focus:** Maintain neural strength and posterior chain
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1. **Romanian Deadlift (barbell)**: 4 × 4–5 @ **RPE 7–8**  
2. **RFESS (DBs)**: 3 × 5–6/side @ **RPE 7**  
3. **Hip Airplane (assisted, slow)**: 2 × 4–6/side  
   *Hip control under rotation—very relevant to ITB history.*
4. **Standing Calf Raise (single-leg)**: 3 × 6–8/side @ **RPE 8**  
5. **Side Plank (heavier/longer)**: 2–3 × 25–40s/side

**Thu — “Upper + Anti-Rotation (Stable)”**
- **Focus:** Upper maintenance; posture/core under fatigue
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1. **Pull-ups (or band-assisted)**: 4 × 4–7 @ **RPE 7–8**
2. **DB Bench Press**: 3 × 6–8 @ **RPE 7–8**
3. **1-Arm DB Row**: 3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 7–8**
4. **Push-up (tempo 2-0-1)**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
   *Adds volume without heavy loading.*
5. **Pallof Press (band)**: 3 × 8–10/side with 2–3s holds
6. **Hip Flexor / T-spine Quick Set:** couch stretch 45s/side + thoracic rotations 6/side

**Sat — “Long Run Companion: Isometrics + Calf/Foot Capacity”**
- **Focus:** Tendon/foot/calf durability; minimal muscle damage
- **Volume:** ~8–14 working sets

1. **Isometric Split Squat Hold (mid-range)**: 3 × 25–40s/side (bodyweight or light DB)  
   *High stimulus, low soreness; great for tendon tolerance and knee/hip control.*
2. **Glute Bridge March (slow)**: 2 × 8–10/side  
   *Posterior chain + pelvis control.*
3. **Calf Raise “Endurance” (single-leg)**: 2 × 12–20/side @ **RPE 7**  
4. **Tibialis Raises**: 2 × 15–25
5. **Farmer Carry**: 3 × 40–60s

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Intensity cap to protect running:** Keep most lower-body work **≤ RPE 8** and stop sets if you feel form degrade or soreness risk rising.
- **Week-to-week:**
  - Tuesday main lifts: add **~2.5–5 lb** when you complete all sets at the top rep target at prescribed RPE.
  - Saturday isometrics: add **5–10s** per hold before adding load.
- **Deload (Week 8):** Reduce lower-body sets by **~50%**; keep calves/feet work light but consistent.
- **Advance to Phase 3** when peak mileage approaches and long runs/tempo work become most important.

---

# Phase 3: Race-Specific Durability + Taper (Weeks 11–15 + Race Week)
**Objective:** Maintain coordination/tendon stiffness and keep hips/calves “online” while reducing fatigue. Strength becomes **minimum effective dose**.

### Weekly Pattern

**Tue — “Touch Strength (Short, Crisp)”**
- **Focus:** Maintain posterior chain + single-leg strength without fatigue
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets

1. **RDL (barbell)**: 2–3 × 4–5 @ **RPE 7**
2. **RFESS (DBs)**: 2 × 5/side @ **RPE 7**
3. **Banded Lateral Walks**: 2 × 10–12 steps/side
4. **Single-Leg Calf Raise**: 2 × 6–10/side @ **RPE 7**
5. **Side Plank**: 2 × 20–30s/side

**Thu — “Upper + Core (Easy)”**
- **Focus:** Posture/shoulders/core; low stress
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

1. **Pull-ups (easy)**: 3 × 3–6 @ **RPE 6–7**
2. **DB Bench Press**: 2–3 × 6–8 @ **RPE 6–7**
3. **1-Arm DB Row**: 2–3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 6–7**
4. **Pallof Press**: 2 × 8–10/side (holds)
5. **Couch stretch**: 1–2 × 45–60s/side

**Sat — “Post-Long Run: Priming / Recovery”**
- **Focus:** Very light activation only (especially as long runs taper)
- **Volume:** ~6–10 working sets

1. **Isometric Split Squat Hold**: 2 × 20–30s/side (light)
2. **Calf Raise (easy pump)**: 2 × 12–15/side @ **RPE 6**
3. **Tibialis Raises**: 1–2 × 15–20
4. **Farmer Carry**: 2 × 40–60s (easy)

### Race Week (Week of Jun 8)
- **Tue:** Do the **Tue session at ~70%** of normal volume (1–2 sets each; keep RPE 6–7).
- **Thu:** Optional **very light** upper + mobility only (20–30 min), or skip if you feel flat.
- **Sat:** **No lifting.** Short shakeout/strides per run plan only.
- **Goal:** arrive with “snap,” not soreness.

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Do not chase load PRs.** Progression is “keep the feel”: crisp reps, no grinding, stop 2–3 reps shy of failure.
- **Fatigue management rule:** If a key run is compromised, the next lift becomes **volume-only** (reduce sets by 30–50%) until running quality stabilizes.
- **Transition to post-race:** 7–14 days of very light strength/mobility, then rebuild to Phase 1 structure.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Order (this cycle):** Phase 1 (4 wks) → Phase 2 (6 wks) → Phase 3 (4–5 wks incl. taper) → Race week taper
- **Triggers to downshift early (move toward Phase 3 earlier):**
  - Mileage reaches **65–70 mpw**, OR
  - Persistent soreness (>24–36 hours), OR
  - Any hint of right ITB flare (lateral knee/hip ache during/after runs)
- **After the marathon:** Return to **Phase 1-style** tissue tolerance for 3–4 weeks, then rebuild.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27 — Initial program created**
  - Built a 3-day supplementary strength plan around Tue/Thu PM and Sat post-long-run AM.
  - Prioritized ITB-risk reduction (hip abduction/control), calf/foot capacity, and trunk stability with low-soreness loading to protect Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun run quality.

--- 

If you share (1) which day your long run typically occurs (it appears to be Saturday), (2) any current working weights for RDL and RFESS, and (3) whether pull-ups are currently unassisted, I can tighten the starting loads and progressions even more precisely.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of February 23, 2026

**Program:** Marathon-Support Strength Plan — Sub-3:15 Build (Durability + Economy)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Tissue Tolerance + Mechanics (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Emily Rodriguez  

## Schedule
- **Mon:** Run-only (key run day per run plan)  
- **Tue:** Hip Dominant + Trunk (Moderate) (home gym, 6:30–7:25 PM)  
- **Wed:** Run-only (key run day per run plan)  
- **Thu:** Upper + Running-Posture Core (Light Legs) (home gym, 6:30–7:25 PM)  
- **Fri:** Run-only (key run day per run plan)  
- **Sat:** Post-Long Run Reset (Very Low Soreness) (home gym, 8:00–8:45 AM)  
- **Sun:** Run-only (key run day per run plan)  

## Week Overview
This is **Week 1** of Phase 1: keep everything **crisp and sub-soreness**, living mostly around **RPE 6–7** so your **Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun run quality stays the priority**. Because we don’t yet have your established working weights, loads are set conservatively; if any set feels easier than the target RPE, add a small bump *that day* (not next week) while staying smooth. Extra attention this week: **right hip stability (ITB risk)** and **hip flexor mobility** (tightness management).

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Hip Dominant + Trunk (Moderate)  
**Location:** Home gym  
**Duration:** ~55 minutes  
**Run today:** Easy run first (per your run plan). Lift after, as scheduled.

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **90/90 hip switches:** 6/side (controlled)
2. **Leg swings (front/back + lateral):** 10/side each direction  
3. **Glute bridge:** 2 × 10 (2-sec squeeze at top)  
4. **Banded lateral walk (light band at ankles):** 1 × 10 steps/side (primer set)  
5. **RDL ramp-up (empty bar):** 1 × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Barbell Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
**Target:** 3 × 6 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 75 lb × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 95 lb × 6 (warm-up; should feel very easy)  
- **Set 4:** 115 lb × 6 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 5:** 115 lb × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 6:** 115 lb × 6 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 2:30–3:00 between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, “hips back,” keep lats tight (bar close). Stop the descent when hamstrings load—don’t chase extra ROM if it tugs your back.

### 2. Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (DBs)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Warm-up:** Bodyweight × 6/side (find stance)  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb DBs (50 lb total) × 6/side (RPE 6)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DBs (50 lb total) × 6/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward torso is fine; keep pelvis level. On the **right side**, think “knee tracks over 2nd–3rd toe” (no inward collapse).

### 3. Banded Lateral Walks
**Target:** 2 × 12–15 steps/side  
- **Set 1:** 15 steps/side  
- **Set 2:** 12 steps/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Toes forward, hips back slightly. You should feel glute med, not hip flexors.

### 4. Side Plank + Top-Leg Abduction
**Target:** 2 × 20–30 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 25 sec/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ribcage stacked, don’t let top hip roll open. Quality > duration.

### 5. Single-Leg Calf Raise (DB held contralateral)
**Target:** 3 × 8–10/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DB × 10/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 25 lb DB × 9/side (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 25 lb DB × 8/side (RPE 7.5—cap here)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 2-sec lower, brief pause at bottom, full big-toe pressure. If plantar fascia feels cranky, shorten range slightly and slow down.

### 6. Short-Foot Drill / Toe Yoga
**Target:** 2 × 35–45 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 40 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 35 sec/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep toes long; don’t curl.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Couch stretch:** 45 sec/side (easy breathing)  
2. **Figure-4 glute stretch:** 30 sec/side  
3. **Calf stretch (knee straight):** 30 sec/side  
4. **Hip flexor “reset” (standing, glute squeezed):** 5 slow breaths/side  

## Notes
- Aim: leave this feeling like you *could have done more*—that’s perfect for Week 1 with key run days protected.  
- If right ITB area feels “hot” on walks/split squats: reduce band tension and shorten split squat ROM slightly next time.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Upper + Running-Posture Core (Light Legs)  
**Location:** Home gym  
**Duration:** ~55 minutes  
**Run today:** Easy run first (per your run plan). Lift after, as scheduled.

## Warm-Up (8 minutes)
1. **Cat-cow:** 6 reps  
2. **Thoracic open books:** 6/side  
3. **Band pull-aparts:** 2 × 12  
4. **Scap push-ups:** 1 × 10  
5. **Light DB bench ramp:** 15 lb DBs × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Pull-ups (band-assisted)
**Target:** 3 × 4–8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Band-assisted pull-up × 6 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** Band-assisted pull-up × 5 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** Band-assisted pull-up × 5 (RPE 7.5)  
**Rest:** 2:00 between sets  
**Notes:** Use a band setup that allows clean reps with no kipping. Full hang to chin-over-bar. If your shoulders feel cranky, swap to **inverted rows** next week.

### 2. Dumbbell Bench Press
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs × 8 (RPE 7)  
- **Set 4:** 30 lb DBs × 7 (RPE 7.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulder blades set; smooth tempo—no grinders.

### 3. 1-Arm DB Row (bench-supported)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb × 10/side (RPE 7)  
- **Set 2:** 35 lb × 10/side (RPE 7)  
- **Set 3:** 35 lb × 9/side (RPE 7.5)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Pull elbow toward hip; pause 1 sec at top to build posture strength.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Overhead Press (DB)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 8/side (RPE 6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 20 lb × 8/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Squeeze glute on the down-knee side; ribs down to avoid back arch.

### 5. Pallof Press (band)
**Target:** 3 × 10–12/side (1–2 sec hold each rep)  
- **Set 1:** 12/side  
- **Set 2:** 10/side  
- **Set 3:** 10/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** You should feel obliques; don’t let band rotate you.

### 6. Hip Flexor Mobility “Finisher” (2 easy rounds)
- **Couch stretch:** 45 sec/side  
- **Glute bridge iso hold:** 35 sec  
**Rest:** Minimal; easy breathing

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. **Doorway pec stretch:** 30 sec/side  
2. **Lat stretch (bench prayer):** 30 sec  
3. **90/90 breathing (feet on bench):** 5 slow breaths  

## Notes
- This day should not affect your legs much—if you notice leg fatigue, check kneeling setup and reduce any unnecessary lower-body bracing.

---

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Post-Long Run Reset (Very Low Soreness)  
**Location:** Home gym  
**Duration:** ~40–45 minutes  
**Run today:** Long run first (per your run plan). Lift after, as scheduled.

## Warm-Up (6 minutes)
1. **2 minutes easy walk + shakeout** (downshift from the long run)  
2. **Ankle rocks (knee-to-wall):** 8/side  
3. **Bodyweight hip hinge (hands on hips):** 10 reps  
4. **Mini-band glute bridge:** 1 × 12

## Main Workout

### 1. Hip Thrust (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6  
- **Set 1:** 65 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 3:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6)  
- **Set 4:** 85 lb × 8 (RPE 6.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Hard glute squeeze at lockout, ribs down. This should feel like “activation + strength,” not a max effort.

### 2. Step-Down (low box, controlled)
**Target:** 2 × 6–8/side @ RPE 6  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 8/side (RPE 6)  
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 6/side (RPE 6; stop before form fades)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slow 3-sec lower, light tap, stand. Watch **right knee** tracking—no hip drop.

### 3. Tibialis Raises (against wall)
**Target:** 2 × 15–20  
- **Set 1:** 20 reps  
- **Set 2:** 18 reps  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Lift toes hard; keep heels planted.

### 4. Farmer Carry (DB/KB)
**Target:** 4 × 30–45 sec @ steady effort  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb DBs × 40 sec  
- **Set 2:** 40 lb DBs × 40 sec  
- **Set 3:** 40 lb DBs × 35 sec  
- **Set 4:** 40 lb DBs × 35 sec  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture, quiet feet. This is “posture under fatigue” for late-marathon form.

### 5. Optional (if time + you feel good): Copenhagen Side Plank (short lever)
**Target:** 1–2 × 15–25 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 15 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** Skip unless you feel very fresh today  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec  
**Notes:** If adductors feel threatened after the long run, skip—no hero reps.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Calf stretch (straight knee):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Soleus stretch (bent knee):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Couch stretch:** 45 sec/side  
4. **Easy supine twist:** 30 sec/side  

## Notes
- Keep this truly “reset.” If you finished the long run depleted, cut one farmer carry set and skip Copenhagen.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Tue (Hinge/Single-leg):** RDL **115×6×3** at RPE 6–7; RFESS **25s×6/side×2**—good starting loads with low soreness risk.  
- **Thu (Upper/Core):** Assisted pull-ups **6/5/5** around RPE 7; DB bench **30s for 8/8/7**—solid posture work without leg cost.  
- **Sat (Post-long run reset):** Hip thrust **85×8×3** stayed easy as intended; step-downs kept controlled and ITB-friendly.  
- **Constraint monitoring:** Right IT band risk managed via moderate unilateral volume + strong form cues; hip flexor tightness addressed Thu + cooldowns—continue daily light couch stretch if it helps.  
- **Next week:** If RDL sets felt clearly ≤RPE 6, bump to **120–125 lb**; if RFESS was easy and stable on the right, move to **30 lb DBs** while keeping reps the same (still RPE 6–7).</Week>
